name: SLB N.V. (formerly Schlumberger Limited)

profile.description: SLB N.V. is the world's largest oilfield services company, holding first or second competitive position in its core service lines globally. Rebranded from Schlumberger in 2022 to signal its evolution toward digital and energy technology services, the company operates across four divisions: Digital & Integration, Reservoir Performance, Well Construction, and Production Systems. Q1 2026 revenue of $8.72B and TTM revenue of approximately $36.7B. CEO Olivier Le Peuch is executing a strategic pivot toward AI-powered digital services, carbon capture, geothermal, and data center solutions.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Innovation Track Record (2/3): SLB's 2022 rebrand from Schlumberger to an 'energy technology company,' combined with the $7.8B ChampionX acquisition (closed 2025) and the launch of its Data Center Solutions business, represent significant business model transformation over the last five years [1][2]. The Production Systems division grew 23% YoY in Q1 2026 to $3.51B, largely driven by ChampionX integration [1]. New energy revenue (CCS, geothermal, critical minerals, data center solutions) exceeded $1B in 2025, ahead of schedule [3]. AI Deployment (2/2): SLB is a leading AI provider for the energy industry — the Delfi cognitive E&P platform is used by 85 of the top 100 global oil producers, and the company launched Lumi generative AI platform and Tela agentic AI assistant purpose-built for upstream energy [2]. Digital & Integration segment achieved ARR of $926M with 32.7% EBITDA margins, the highest-margin business in the portfolio [3]. SLB's AI tools have delivered a documented 23.4% efficiency improvement in customer operations [3]. Market Share (2/2): SLB is the undisputed global leader in oilfield services, holding first or second position in every core service line worldwide. The company operates in 100+ countries with the broadest geographic footprint in the industry [2]. Product Pipeline (1/2): SLB will host a Digital Investor Day on June 17, 2026, to outline its AI and digital growth strategy [2]. Data Center Solutions is scaling rapidly and the $10B new energy revenue target by 2040 provides a long-term growth vector, though specific near-term product launch dates and revenue targets are limited. R&D Intensity (1/1): R&D spending of approximately $730M annually (~2% of revenue) is in line with oilfield services industry peers and above most E&P companies, reflecting SLB's technology-first positioning [4].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): SLB's purpose — 'driving energy innovation for a balanced planet' and 'unlocking access to energy for the benefit of all' — is specific and thoughtful, connecting the company's work to a larger purpose beyond profit [5]. The 2022 rebrand demonstrated willingness to challenge its own 96-year-old identity in service of this purpose. Strategic decisions — investing in CCS, geothermal, and digital before proven returns — trace back to this mission. However, the purpose has not yet been tested through decades of consistent execution under this framing; the transformation is only four years old. Community & Charitable Impact (1/2): SLB has set documented Scope 1/2 emissions reduction targets of 30% by end of 2025, operates sustainability programs across its global footprint, and the ChampionX acquisition advances ESG-related chemical management capabilities [3]. However, community investment programs focus on supplier diversity and workforce development rather than distinctive large-scale philanthropy. Employee Retention (0/2): SLB's workforce of 109,000 employees declined modestly from 110,000 in 2025 (-0.91%), which may reflect efficiency initiatives rather than attrition concerns [6]. Executive compensation is structured around long-term equity retention. However, no specific voluntary turnover rates, average tenure data, or employee satisfaction metrics are publicly available. Insider Buying (0/2): All seven recent insider transactions in 2026 were stock sales totaling $10.4M, including EVP Steve Matthew Gassen selling 53,379 shares ($3.0M) on May 1, 2026, and director Patrick de La Chevardiere selling 2,000 shares on May 7, 2026 [7]. No net insider buying identified in the last 12 months.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics. Q1 2026 revenue of $8.72B was up 3% YoY, with Production Systems leading at $3.51B (+23% from ChampionX) while Well Construction declined 6% to $2.8B due to Middle East disruptions [1]. D/E of 42.5% is well below the 70% target, reflecting conservative balance sheet management. ROE of 14.1% narrowly misses the 15% target but remains solid for an industrial company. Forward P/E of 16.74 is attractive relative to the 20 target, suggesting reasonable valuation for a technology-oriented energy services leader. Profit margin of 9.3% is the primary weakness, falling well below the 20% target due to the capital-intensive nature of oilfield services. The dividend has been raised for five consecutive years since 2022 with a cumulative 136% increase, and the 5-year dividend growth CAGR of approximately 18% is exceptional [8]. Payout ratio of ~52% is manageable given the company's $4B+ annual shareholder return commitment. SLB returned $4.02B to shareholders in 2025 and has committed to exceeding $4B in 2026 [1].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): SLB's core business — providing specialized oilfield services, subsurface imaging, drilling technology, well construction, and production chemistry — requires deep physical-world expertise, proprietary downhole tools, and on-site operations that AI structurally cannot replace [2]. The company operates across 100+ countries in extreme environments (deepwater, high-pressure/high-temperature reservoirs, arctic conditions) that demand physical infrastructure and human expertise far beyond AI's capabilities. Moreover, SLB is itself an AI infrastructure provider for the energy industry: the Delfi platform serves 85 of the top 100 global oil producers, Lumi provides generative AI for E&P workflows, and Tela is an agentic AI assistant automating upstream processes [2]. The growing Data Center Solutions business means AI boom-driven demand for energy directly benefits SLB. CEO Le Peuch has described AI as 'the X factor for our industry,' positioning SLB as the enabler rather than the disrupted [2]. AI Opportunity (1/1): SLB generates meaningful and growing AI-related revenue — Digital & Integration segment ARR of $926M with 32.7% EBITDA margins, total digital-related annual revenue approaching $3B, and the Data Center Solutions business scaling rapidly [3]. A Digital Investor Day on June 17, 2026, will further detail the AI revenue roadmap [2].

infinite_game.summary: SLB's 2022 rebrand from the 96-year-old Schlumberger identity to an energy technology company is a genuine act of Existential Flexibility in service of an evolving Just Cause. CEO Le Peuch's commitment to carbon capture, geothermal, and digital services before these markets have proven commercial returns demonstrates infinite thinking. However, the transformation is still early — the new purpose has not been tested through a major crisis or leadership transition, and core fossil fuel services remain the vast majority of revenue. Sinek would see a company with genuine infinite aspirations whose resolve has not yet been fully tested.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): SLB's evolving purpose — 'driving energy innovation for a balanced planet' and 'unlocking access to energy for the benefit of all' — is genuine, distinctive, and increasingly shapes strategic decisions [5]. The 2022 rebrand, the $1B+ new energy revenue milestone, and investments in CCS and geothermal before proven commercial returns all trace back to this cause. However, the purpose in its current articulation is only four years old and has not yet been tested through a major crisis, leadership transition, or sustained market downturn. Sinek would recognize genuine intentionality but would want to see multi-decade validation before calling this a Just Cause that IS the competitive advantage.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): SLB operates one of the most multinational workforces in the industrial sector — 109,000 employees across 100+ countries with deep local expertise [6]. CEO Le Peuch has been at SLB for over 30 years and became CEO in 2019, reflecting a promote-from-within culture that builds institutional trust. The 2022 rebrand was managed without major organizational disruption or mass departures. Board governance ratings are among the highest in the energy sector [2]. However, no specific employee turnover data, satisfaction metrics, or evidence of exceptional psychological safety is publicly available. The modest workforce reduction (110,000 to 109,000) suggests ongoing efficiency optimization typical of the industry.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): SLB explicitly benchmarks against Halliburton, Baker Hughes, and emerging technology-focused energy service companies. The digital pivot was partly inspired by recognition that technology companies would disrupt legacy field services if incumbents didn't adapt — using rivals' strengths to clarify its own strategic direction rather than simply trying to outspend competitors [2]. Management commentary focuses on building differentiated capabilities rather than defeating specific competitors.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (2/2): SLB's 2022 rebrand from the world-famous Schlumberger name — voluntarily abandoning a 96-year-old identity to become an energy technology company — is a textbook act of existential flexibility [2]. Combined with the $7.8B ChampionX acquisition pivoting into production chemistry, the $1B+ new energy revenue from CCS/geothermal/critical minerals, and the Data Center Solutions business, SLB is deliberately building a new identity while its legacy oilfield services business still generates the vast majority of revenue. The pivot is driven by the evolving Just Cause, not desperation — SLB is the market leader in oilfield services and chose to transform from a position of strength.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Investing in carbon capture, geothermal, and critical minerals before proven commercial returns required institutional courage, as did voluntarily rebranding away from the globally recognized Schlumberger name [2]. The $7.8B ChampionX acquisition was a significant bet on production chemistry and digital-enabled chemical management. However, the company's core fossil fuel services business remains the overwhelming majority of revenue (~97%), suggesting the transformation involves measured, incremental courage rather than the dramatic, costly commitment Sinek looks for. The $4B+ annual shareholder return commitment also constrains the capital available for transformative investment.

investment_thesis: SLB is the world's largest oilfield services company executing a credible transformation into an energy technology leader, trading at an attractive 16.74x forward earnings with a growing 2.1% dividend yield and $4B+ annual shareholder return commitment [1][8]. Q1 2026 revenue of $8.72B grew 3% YoY, led by Production Systems at $3.51B (+23% from ChampionX integration) [1]. The digital business is the crown jewel — Digital & Integration ARR of $926M at 32.7% EBITDA margins represents a structurally higher-value business than traditional field services, and the Delfi platform's adoption by 85 of the top 100 global oil producers creates a sticky, recurring revenue base [3]. New energy revenue exceeding $1B (CCS, geothermal, critical minerals, data center solutions) validates the long-term transformation thesis, and the upcoming Digital Investor Day on June 17, 2026, should provide a catalyst for investor re-rating of the digital business [2]. The balance sheet is conservatively managed at 42.5% D/E with strong free cash flow generation.
thesis_continued: The primary headwind is oilfield activity cyclicality and near-term earnings pressure. Q1 2026 net income declined 6% YoY to $752M, Well Construction revenue fell 6% due to Middle East disruptions, and adjusted EBITDA declined 12% YoY — a concerning deterioration in the core business's profitability [1]. Profit margin of 9.3% is structurally low for a company positioning itself as a technology leader, and ROE of 14.1% falls just short of the 15% quality threshold. The digital transformation, while genuine, is incomplete — Digital ARR of $926M remains less than 3% of total revenue, meaning traditional cyclical oilfield services still dominate the earnings profile. Any sustained oil price weakness below $60/bbl could accelerate upstream spending cuts by national oil companies and IOCs, compressing SLB's revenue and margins by 15-25%. Geopolitical exposure across Latin America, Middle East, and Africa (>75% of revenue is international) adds complexity, and insider selling totaling $10.4M in early 2026 with no insider buying is a negative signal [7].
key_catalysts: Digital Investor Day on June 17, 2026, could catalyze investor re-rating of SLB's digital business by providing specific ARR growth targets, margin expansion roadmap, and AI revenue visibility beyond the current $926M base [2]

ChampionX integration delivering synergies — the acquisition is expected to generate $400M in annual pretax synergies within three years and be accretive to EPS in 2026, providing a tailwind to earnings growth independent of oilfield activity levels [1]

International offshore and conventional exploration spending recovery — return to 2019 activity levels would add $2-3B to SLB's revenue at high incremental margins, leveraging the fixed-cost digital platform and expanded Production Systems capabilities
key_risks: Oilfield services cyclicality — Q1 2026 adjusted EBITDA already declined 12% YoY and Well Construction revenue fell 6% due to Middle East disruptions; sustained oil prices below $60/bbl could trigger 15-25% earnings compression as upstream operators cut exploration and production spending [1]

Digital transformation execution risk — Digital ARR of $926M represents less than 3% of total revenue, meaning the technology re-rating thesis depends on scaling a relatively small business while the core cyclical franchise faces near-term headwinds; the transformation may take 5-10 years to meaningfully shift SLB's margin profile [3]

Geopolitical concentration — over 75% of revenue is generated internationally across Latin America, Middle East, and Africa, exposing SLB to currency risk, sanctions complexity, and potential disruptions from regional conflicts as evidenced by Q1 2026 Middle East-related revenue impacts [1]
